title: Jinja 3.1.6 Environments, Layouts, Autoescape, and Contracts · Runtime Lens: Jinja Environment
summary: Templates are constructed from author text, autoescape is assumed rather than configured, missing values render empty, safe filters spread, inheritance cycles hide layout ownership, and logic consumes arbitrary objects. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the jinja environment increment in strict autoescaped layout and component system. Learners pr…
objectives: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.
Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-templates-jinja-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-templates-jinja-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_templates_jinja_2(templates: int = 6, rendered: int = 6, autoescaped: bool = True, strict_undefined: bool = True, unsafe_safe_filters: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if templates < 1 or rendered < 0:
        return False, rendered, "template-count"
    if rendered != templates:
        return False, rendered, "template-coverage"
    if not autoescaped or not strict_undefined:
        return False, rendered, "environment-policy"
    if unsafe_safe_filters > 0:
        return False, unsafe_safe_filters, "safe-boundary"
    assert rendered == templates
    return True, rendered, "strict-layout-system"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-jinja-environment-1o1sxe
# Competency: ssg-jinja-environment.
# Case 7986: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_jinja_environment_1o1sxe(templates: int = 6, rendered: int = 6, autoescaped: bool = True, strict_undefined: bool = True, unsafe_safe_filters: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1o1sxe = "1o1sxe-exs1o1-7986"
    assert evidence_variant_1o1sxe
    evidence_axes_1o1sxe = (
        "reader1o1sxe",
        "source7986",
        "parseexs1o1",
        "route1o1sxe7986",
        "artifact79861o1sxe",
        "recoveryexs1o17986",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1o1sxe)) == 6
    if templates < 1 or rendered < 0:
        return False, rendered, "template-count"
    if rendered != templates:
        return False, rendered, "template-coverage"
    if not autoescaped or not strict_undefined:
        return False, rendered, "environment-policy"
    if unsafe_safe_filters > 0:
        return False, unsafe_safe_filters, "safe-boundary"
    assert rendered == templates
    return True, rendered, "strict-layout-system"

Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the jinja environment defect evidence boundary.

For repair the jinja environment defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Jinja Environment model · Theory Jinja Environment
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Jinja Environment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4675 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to configure strict autoescaped templates with explicit view-model and safe-markup boundaries; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Jinja Environment model · Theory Jinja Environment.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Jinja Environment model · Theory Jinja Environment, keep Jinja Environment connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Jinja Environment model · Theory Jinja Environment, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Jinja Environment precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Jinja Environment model · Theory Jinja Environment, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 4675 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to configure strict autoescaped templates with explicit view-model and safe-markup boundaries; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one configured Jinja environment with bounded loader roots, explicit autoescape, StrictUndefined, deterministic globals, and version evidence.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Jinja Environment model · Theory Jinja Environment, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Jinja enables safe HTML autoescaping for every template by default.
